codeReview.summary: The implementation covers the requested edge-resilience behaviors: no-text/scanned PDF rejection with a user-facing message, sanitized corrupted-PDF error handling, race mitigation for rapid repeated drops via an AbortController gate, and 300→240 DPI fallback warning surfaced on the done screen.

issues.description: The processing gate controller is not cleared on the successful DETECTION_COMPLETE path, so getProcessingGate().isProcessing() can remain true after processing finishes. This does not currently break the feature flow, but it leaves gate state inaccurate for future callers/tests that rely on isProcessing().

sharedStateObservations.observation: Architecture documentation has a stale app-state enum and omits the ERROR state, which is now central to edge-case handling (corrupted/scanned flows return to drop screen via ERROR).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:147 lists type AppState = 'IDLE' | 'LOADING' | 'NEEDS_REVIEW' | 'PROCESSING' | 'DONE', while src/app/state.ts:27 includes '| 'ERROR''.

summary: Reviewed feature edge-case-resilience for hardening using feature metadata, handoff, commit diff, transcript skeleton, and skill guidance. Result is PASS: required behaviors are implemented and validated in code, with one non-blocking gate-state accuracy issue noted plus one shared-state documentation gap.
